cases.description: Always rounds away from zero, regardless of the discarded digit
For negative numbers, 'up' means away from zero (more negative), not toward positive infinity
Negative num_digits rounds to the left of the decimal point (nearest multiple of 10 here)
cases.expected_note: Rounding 21.5 up (away from zero) to the nearest 10 gives 30 -- this is Microsoft's own documented example for ROUNDUP

cases.description: Rounds up to 2 decimal places even though the 3rd decimal (1) would round down normally
